Eucalyptus globulus, commonly known as the Tasmanian blue gum or blue gum, is a plant species in the myrtle family, native to Australia. The health benefits of Eucalyptus globulus include its ability to relieve respiratory issues, such as congestion and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Eucalyptus globulus include eucalyptol, a volatile oil that is responsible for its therapeutic properties, as well as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The most common herbal preparations made with Eucalyptus globulus include essential oils, teas, and topical creams, which are used to treat respiratory issues, skin irritations, and other health conditions.
